perturbation.original_text: This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time. However, Watchit retains the right to terminate this agreement at any point, with or without cause, by providing a 30-day written notice to Oceanic Time Warner Cable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text implies a fixed term until the end of 2006. The added sentence introduces a clause that Watchit can terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days' notice, contradicting the implied fixed term. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will be able to not include any content that it deems inappropriate or distasteful. This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time. However, Watchit retains the right to terminate this agreement at any point, with or without cause, by providing a 30-day written notice to Oceanic Time Warner Cable. Both parties agree to discuss use of information gathered form this arrangement before using it in any kind of promotional or public message.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement states the agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006. The changed text adds a clause allowing Watchit to terminate the agreement at any point with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the initial fixed term stated in the original agreement and now in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Watchit shall have the exclusive right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content and will have the right to brand the content under the Watchit brand and place a "bug" on the screen identifying the content with a Watchit trademark.
perturbation.changed_text: Watchit shall have the exclusive right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content and will have the right to brand the content under the Watchit brand and place a "bug" on the screen identifying the content with a Watchit trademark. Oceanic Time Warner Cable reserves the right to refuse advertising from companies that are in direct competition with Time Warner Cable's advertisers, therefore watchit will not have the exclusive rights.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that Watchit has the exclusive right to sell third-party advertising. The additional statement that Oceanic Time Warner Cable reserves the right to refuse ads from competing companies introduces a limitation on Watchit's exclusive right, creating an in-text contradiction. This creates uncertainty about who truly has the final say in advertising sales.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Watchit shall have the exclusive right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content and will have the right to brand the content under the Watchit brand and place a "bug" on the screen identifying the content with a Watchit trademark. Oceanic Time Warner Cable reserves the right to refuse advertising from companies that are in direct competition with Time Warner Cable's advertisers, therefore watchit will not have the exclusive rights. Oceanic Time Warner Cable will include the following disclaimer on the Watchit Content: "The materials shown are for entertainment purposes only and does not provide any guarantees of winning or improving your odds of winning on this program. The rules of the games as shown may not apply to all properties and may change from time to time."
perturbation.justification: The original text gives Watchit the exclusive right to sell advertising. The changed text adds a condition where Oceanic Time Warner Cable can refuse ads from competitors, meaning Watchit's right to sell advertising is no longer exclusive, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text section is identical to the changed text section, and the changed text directly contradicts itself by first stating that Watchit has exclusive rights, and then stating that Watchit doesn't have exclusive rights, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.
